In 2002, Cohle independently investigates a series of old missing persons cases, discovering connections to Billy Lee Tuttle's Wellspring Foundation, which financed rural religious schools. He tracks down the former preacher Theriot, now a drunk who quit the ministry, and learns that Theriot found photographs of nude sleeping children in Tuttle's possession. Theriot reported this to Deacon Farrar, who dismissed the concern. Theriot implies he was intimidated into silence after being questioned in 1995.

Cohle visits Kelly Reider, the girl rescued from the Ledoux compound in 1995, now institutionalized with regressive catatonia. When asked if a third man was involved in her abduction, she describes a giant with scars and begins screaming when Cohle asks about the man's face.

Cohle interrogates a woman guilty of infanticide via Munchausen by Proxy, coldly suggesting she should kill herself. Marty refuses to engage, deepening their friction.

Meanwhile, Marty confronts and beats two young men found with his underage daughter Audrey, abusing his authority as a detective. He later starts an affair with Beth, a former Ranch worker he once helped years ago. Maggie discovers evidence of Marty's infidelity — clothes in the washer, images on his phone — and realizes he is cheating again. After a failed attempt to sleep with a stranger at a bar, she goes to Rust's apartment late at night and seduces him while he is vulnerable.

Maggie tells Marty about the encounter. Marty attacks Rust in the police station parking lot, beating him badly. Rust abruptly quits the force.

In 2012, Marty leaves the station after refusing to believe Papania and Gilbough's claims about Rust's involvement. Maggie is interviewed and defends Rust's character. The episode closes with Rust's truck pulling up behind Marty's car in 2012. They exchange terse greetings and Rust suggests getting a drink.
